header_summary:
Bitcoin Core version 31.1 has been launched, featuring performance upgrades, bug fixes, and enhanced privacy measures, such as a resolution to an IP address leak issue and improved handling of transaction data and networking protocols. This new version supports various operating systems and is available for download from [Bitcoin Core's official website](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/). Users are encouraged to update from any previous versions, even those that have reached end-of-life, by following specific installation procedures for Windows, macOS, and Linux. 

The development of a qbit pool incorporates post-quantum signatures and uses a TIDES-style share accounting system, with the primary implementation available at [Prism Pool](https://prismpool.io). This model operates efficiently through direct coinbase outputs and CTV fanouts, balancing transaction fee predictability and economic viability without the need for funded relayers. The implementation and source code are detailed further on [GitHub](https://github.com/Qbit-Org/qbit-mining-bootstrap) and discussed in the context of fee structures at [Scaling Noncustodial Mining Payouts with CTV](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/scaling-noncustodial-mining-payouts-with-ctv/1753).

In a separate analysis, the dynamic block size proposal for Bitcoin transactions highlights potential impacts on blockchain efficiency due to varying transaction types and fee levels. The study suggests that higher fee scenarios could significantly reduce block sizes, affecting network throughput, and discusses the integration of post-quantum cryptographic signatures, which could alter average transaction sizes and fees. This comprehensive discussion is accessible in detail at [Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/addressing-the-diminishing-block-subsidy/2640/19).

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 31.1 has been released with important improvements and bug fixes.
- The upgrade requires shutting down older versions completely before installation.
- The new version enhances system stability, security, and includes privacy improvements.

recent_posts.title: Fee Selection for CTV-Based Mining Pool Fanouts
recent_posts.summary:
- The qbit pool utilizes post-quantum signatures and OP_CTV on a UTXO chain.
- Payouts are managed through direct coinbase outputs and CTV fanouts, maintaining economic thresholds.
- Fee setting challenges arise, with alternatives focusing on predictability and economic viability.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 31.1 introduces enhancements and bug fixes to improve security and functionality.
- The upgrade process requires shutting down the current software version before installation.
- This release supports major operating systems and has resolved key issues in database and privacy features.

recent_posts.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
recent_posts.summary:
- The analysis reviews Bitcoin's block size and transaction fees impact on blockchain efficiency.
- Higher fees could reduce block sizes from 1MB to approximately 418KB, impacting network throughput.
- Post-quantum signature adoption and scaling technology effectiveness depend on transaction demand.

active_posts.title: DahLIAS: Discrete Logarithm-Based Interactive Aggregate Signatures
active_posts.summary: - DahLIAS introduces a breakthrough constant-size aggregate signature compatible with secp256k1.  - DahLIAS supports efficient signing and faster verification, enhancing Cross-Input Signature Aggregation.  - The scheme maintains security without key tweaking and improves multi-signature operations.

active_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin developers explore new security features for P2TRv2 to counter quantum threats.
- Two proposals, Tripwire and Miner Lockdown, aim to automatically disable EC opcodes.
- Integration of these mechanisms into consensus rules promises reliable security enhancements.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: Full-Aggregation of BIP 340 Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal suggests enhanced signature aggregation.
- It was developed by Jonas Nick, Tim Ruffing, and Yannick Seurin.
- Stakeholders are encouraged to provide feedback on its implementation.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary:
- SegData applies a soft-fork for data storage in Bitcoin blocks using a separate Merkle root.
- It enables efficient, prunable storage with node-based validation and optional data retention.
- Implementation issues include version slot distribution and data retrieval privacy.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal enhances BIP-360 by optimizing EC signature recovery in a P2MR structure.
- It proposes eliminating the EC public key script to reduce witness size effectively.
- Additional nonce generation steps are introduced to safeguard against security vulnerabilities.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy focuses on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations rather than Layer 1.
- Recent technologies aim to optimize rather than transform protocol-level privacy.
- Regulatory compatibility and non-invasiveness guide current Bitcoin privacy enhancements.

today_in_history_posts.title: Updating the Scaling Roadmap
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Paul Storzc finds Greg Maxwell's Bitcoin scaling roadmap outdated and seeks an update.
- Storzc's new draft roadmap focuses on specific improvements and transaction processing enhancements.
- He suggests updating the roadmap is beneficial even for those who might not endorse it.

today_in_history_posts.title: BOLT 11, real time micro payments, and route redundancy
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The writer queries the absence of a refund address in BOLT 11, unlike BIP 70.
- They discuss adapting a fuel pump for Lightning with necessary refund functionalities.
- Concerns about real-time micropayments' feasibility and reliable delivery routes are raised.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin scalability involves creating 50,000 off-chain entities for user transactions.
- New "bitcoin banks" would enhance security and afford scalability via interconnected services.
- Layer 2 solutions like Lightning Network require sound fee management for effectiveness.
